word: H5875 - עֵין הַקֹּורֵא - ʻÊyn haq-Qôwrêʼ - ane-hak-ko-ray' - En-hakhore

sections.paragraph:
Original: עין הקּורא

Transliteration: ‛êyn haqqôrê'

Phonetic: ane-hak-ko-ray'

BDB Definition:

En-hakkore = "spring of One calling"
  1. the spring which the Lord brought forth in response to Samson's complaint of being thirsty after he had just killed 1000 men with the jawbone of an ass

Origin: from H5869 and the act part of H7121

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
